channel.title: 21-DAY Daniel Fast
channel.description: This 21-day journey can be started at any time of the year. Whenever you feel the call to reset, refocus, or draw closer to God, these days are here to guide you with Scripture, reflection, prayer, and simple daily practices. The structure comes from the example of Daniel, who set his heart to seek God for three weeks. These 21 days invite you into a rhythm of consistency and spiritual focus.
channel.author: Eric Kouassi
channel.summary: The Daniel Fast is a partial fast rooted in Scripture. It emphasizes simple, plant-based foods and a posture of humility before God.

funding.text: Support the Show by Buying Me a Coffee

episodes.title: Day 1: Setting Your Heart to Seek God | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Daniel 10:2)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 1 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Setting Your Heart to Seek God
Scripture: Daniel 10:2 – "I, Daniel, had been in mourning for three weeks."

Reflection: Today marks the beginning of a 21-day journey of intention and openness. Daniel created space to hear God more clearly. You begin with the same simple yes.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: A Simple Yes to God's Invitation
episodes.summary: In Daniel 10:2, we see Daniel intentionally setting aside distractions and comforts—mourning and abstaining for three weeks—to position himself to receive deeper understanding from God. This opening day invites you to do the same: start with humble intention, quiet your heart, and say 'yes' to seeking God more earnestly over the next 21 days. Your journey of openness begins now.

episodes.title: Day 2: Choosing Simplicity Again | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Daniel 10:3)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 2 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Choosing Simplicity Again
Scripture: Daniel 10:3 – "I ate no rich food, no meat or wine entered my mouth."

Reflection: Daniel’s practice was simple, not extreme. Simplicity clears the heart. Today is an invitation to choose less so you can receive more.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Embracing Less to Gain More
episodes.summary: Daniel 10:3 reveals Daniel's deliberate choice to abstain from rich foods, meat, and wine during his three-week season of seeking God. This wasn't about rigid rules but about creating inner space—simplifying his diet and habits to sharpen his spiritual focus and openness to divine insight. Today, you're invited to echo that choice: release one area of excess or distraction, not out of obligation, but with peaceful intention. In letting go of 'more,' you make room to receive God's presence and guidance more fully as this 21-day journey continues.

episodes.title: Day 3: Making Space for God | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Psalm 46:10)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 3 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Making Space for God
Scripture: Psalm 46:10 – "Be still, and know that I am God."

Reflection: Stillness is not empty—it is spacious. When you pause, you make room for God to speak and move.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Be Still and Know
episodes.summary: Psalm 46:10 invites us into a profound truth: true knowing of God often begins not in striving, but in stillness. Daniel's 21 days of seeking included creating space—through mourning, simplicity, and now intentional quiet—to let God's voice rise above the noise. Today, pause and practice the countercultural act of stopping. In one minute of silence, release the rush, slow your breath, and open your attention to God's presence. Stillness isn't absence; it's the fertile ground where you begin to truly know that He is God. As this journey deepens, let these quiet moments become doorways to greater awareness and intimacy with Him.

episodes.title: Day 4: The Slow Work of Grace | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (James 5:7)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 4 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: The Slow Work of Grace
Scripture: James 5:7 – "Be patient, therefore, beloved, until the coming of the Lord."

Reflection: Grace works slowly, like seeds growing underground. Trust that God is moving even when you cannot see it.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Trusting the Unseen Growth
episodes.summary: James 5:7 calls us to patient waiting, comparing it to a farmer who waits for the precious fruit of the earth with steady hope. In Daniel’s 21 days of seeking, answers did not come instantly; grace often works beneath the surface, hidden from view, like seeds taking root in dark soil. Today, you are invited to release the pressure to see immediate results. Slow down, trust God’s timing, and practice patience in one small way—whether in a conversation, a chore, or your own heart. What feels like delay is often divine cultivation. As this journey unfolds, let patience become your companion, opening you to receive the deeper fruit God is growing in and through you.

episodes.title: Day 5: When Desire Feels Small | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Matthew 5:6)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 5 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: When Desire Feels Small
Scripture: Matthew 5:6 – "Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled."

Reflection: Some days your desire for God feels small. Offer it anyway. God can work with even the faintest hunger.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Offering Even the Smallest Hunger
episodes.summary: In Matthew 5:6, Jesus blesses those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, promising they will be filled. This beatitude does not require a roaring appetite—only an honest longing, however faint. Daniel’s 21 days began with intention, not perfect passion; God met him in his steady turning toward Him. Today, acknowledge whatever desire you carry—even if it feels weak, distracted, or barely present—and offer it to God without shame. Speak the simple prayer of invitation: 'Lord, increase my desire for You.' In bringing your small hunger honestly before Him, you open the door for grace to kindle and deepen it. As this journey continues, trust that God receives every sincere reach and faithfully satisfies the soul that turns toward Him.

episodes.title: Day 6: Staying Faithful in the Ordinary | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Luke 16:10)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 6 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Staying Faithful in the Ordinary
Scripture: Luke 16:10 – "Whoever is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much."

Reflection: Holiness grows in ordinary moments. Faithfulness is built through small, steady choices.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Faithful in the Little Things
episodes.summary: Luke 16:10 reveals a profound principle of spiritual growth: God values consistency in the small, unseen moments more than dramatic gestures. Daniel’s 21 days were not filled with constant miracles or visions from the start; they were marked by steady, ordinary faithfulness—day after day of turning toward God amid routine life. Today, you are invited to embrace the ordinary as sacred ground. Choose one everyday task—washing dishes, answering an email, walking to work, or listening to someone—and do it with full presence, love, and intention as an offering to Jesus. These small acts of fidelity quietly build the muscle of perseverance and open your heart to receive more of God’s ‘much.’ As this journey continues, let faithfulness in the little become the foundation for deeper trust and greater fruit in the days ahead.

episodes.title: Day 7: Resting in God’s Presence | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Matthew 11:28)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 7 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Resting in God’s Presence
Scripture: Matthew 11:28 – "Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest."

Reflection: Rest is not laziness—it is trust. God invites you to rest in Him, not to earn His love but to receive it.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Come and Find Rest
episodes.summary: In Matthew 11:28, Jesus extends one of the most compassionate invitations in Scripture: Come to Me—all who are weary and burdened—and I will give you rest. After a week of intentional turning, simplifying, stilling, waiting, offering desire, and staying faithful, today God invites you to pause and simply receive. Rest is an act of trust, not a reward for performance. Daniel’s long season of seeking included moments of quiet dependence; so does this journey. Whether through a slow walk, sitting with a warm drink, or simply breathing in God’s nearness, let yourself be held. Release the need to strive or produce. In resting in His presence, you allow peace to settle over your soul and make space for His love to renew you from the inside out. As these 21 days continue, may rest become a rhythm that carries you deeper into intimacy with Him.

episodes.title: Day 8: Naming What Distracts You | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Psalm 139:23)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 8 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Naming What Distracts You
Scripture: Psalm 139:23 – "Search me, O God, and know my heart."

Reflection: You cannot release what you have not named. God gently reveals what pulls your heart away.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.subtitle: Search Me, O God
episodes.summary: Psalm 139:23 is a brave and trusting prayer: 'Search me, O God, and know my heart.' The psalmist invites divine light into hidden places, not out of fear, but out of longing for deeper communion. In Daniel’s season of seeking, clarity came as he remained open to God’s probing presence. Today, you are gently invited to do the same. Pause and ask God to reveal one thing—perhaps a habit, worry, screen time, comparison, or inner noise—that quietly pulls your attention away from Him. Name it honestly, without judgment, and write it down as an act of surrender. Naming is the first step toward freedom; what is brought into the light can be released into God’s caring hands. As this journey moves into its second week, let this courageous honesty become a pathway to clearer attention and renewed closeness with Him.

episodes.title: Day 9: Letting Go of False Strength | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (2 Corinthians 12:9)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 9 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Letting Go of False Strength
Scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 – "My grace is sufficient for you, for power is made perfect in weakness."

Reflection: You don’t have to be strong for God. He meets you in weakness and fills you with His strength.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Grace in Our Weakness
episodes.summary: In 2 Corinthians 12:9, Paul shares God’s tender response to his plea for relief: 'My grace is sufficient for you, for power is made perfect in weakness.' This is not a call to perform strength but an invitation to release it. Daniel’s 21 days of seeking were not marked by self-reliant power; they were sustained by humble dependence on God amid limitation and longing. Today, you are gently encouraged to stop pretending—to name one place where you feel inadequate, tired, uncertain, or simply not enough—and offer it honestly to the Lord. Speak it aloud or in your heart: 'Here is my weakness; be my strength.' In that honest surrender, God’s grace rushes in—not to fix you instantly, but to fill you with His perfect power. As this journey continues, let weakness become a doorway rather than a barrier, drawing you into deeper reliance on the One whose strength is made complete precisely where ours ends.

episodes.title: Day 10: The Gift of Spiritual Hunger | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Psalm 42:1)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 10 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: The Gift of Spiritual Hunger
Scripture: Psalm 42:1 – "As the deer longs for streams of water, so my soul longs for you, O God."

Reflection: Spiritual hunger is not a problem—it is a gift. It draws you toward God.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Longing as a Sacred Gift
episodes.summary: Psalm 42:1 paints a beautiful picture of the soul’s deep thirst: 'As the deer longs for streams of water, so my soul longs for you, O God.' The psalmist does not hide or suppress this longing—he names it as the truest expression of his need for God. In Daniel’s 21 days, hunger—both physical and spiritual—became the pathway to greater revelation. Today, you are invited to reframe any sense of emptiness or yearning as a holy gift, not a flaw. Rather than fighting or ignoring spiritual hunger, welcome it as evidence that your soul is alive and oriented toward its true Source. Before your next meal, pause and give thanks for this deeper hunger of the heart. Let it draw you nearer to God with honesty and hope. As this journey moves forward, may your longing become a steady compass, guiding you into ever-closer communion with the One who alone can satisfy.

episodes.title: Day 11: When Prayer Feels Dry | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Psalm 63:1)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 11 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: When Prayer Feels Dry
Scripture: Psalm 63:1 – "My soul thirsts for you; my flesh faints for you."

Reflection: Dryness in prayer is not failure. It is an invitation to trust God beyond feelings.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Trusting Beyond the Feelings
episodes.summary: Psalm 63:1 captures David’s raw longing in a barren wilderness: 'My soul thirsts for you; my flesh faints for you.' Even when words feel empty and emotions are flat, the psalmist still turns toward God. Dryness in prayer is a common part of the spiritual journey—not a sign of distance or defeat, but an opportunity to persevere in love rather than in feeling. Daniel’s 21 days included long stretches before any vision came; faithfulness persisted through silence. Today, you are invited to stay present with God for just two minutes, even if prayer feels mechanical or joyless. Simply offer the time itself—no need for eloquence or fervor. In showing up amid dryness, you declare that your trust is anchored not in your experience, but in God’s unchanging nearness. As this journey deepens, may these quiet, persistent moments strengthen your root in Him, preparing your heart for the refreshment that comes in His perfect timing.

episodes.title: Day 12: Purifying Intentions | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Psalm 51:10)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 12 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Purifying Intentions
Scripture: Psalm 51:10 – "Create in me a clean heart, O God."

Reflection: God purifies your intentions gently. He brings you back to the ‘why’ behind your devotion.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Create in Me a Clean Heart
episodes.summary: Psalm 51:10 is David’s heartfelt cry after deep failure: 'Create in me a clean heart, O God.' He does not ask for a quick fix but for divine renewal from the inside out. In Daniel’s 21 days of seeking, his pursuit was marked by humble, purified intention—mourning, fasting, and turning wholly toward God without mixed motives. Today, you are gently invited to return to the root of this journey: Why are you here? Pause and honestly ask yourself, 'Why am I seeking God today?' Write your answer—no need for perfection, only truth. Offer whatever you discover—whether pure longing, mixed motives, weariness, or hope—into God’s purifying hands. He does not condemn; He cleanses and renews. As this second week unfolds, let this simple act of reflection realign your heart, making your devotion lighter, truer, and more wholly directed toward Him who alone can create what is clean and new.

episodes.title: Day 13: The Quiet Conversion | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Mark 4:27)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 13 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: The Quiet Conversion
Scripture: Mark 4:27 – "The seed sprouts and grows, though he does not know how."

Reflection: Most transformation is quiet. God works beneath the surface, shaping your heart in hidden ways.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: The Seed That Grows in Secret
episodes.summary: In Mark 4:27, Jesus describes the mystery of the kingdom: the seed sprouts and grows, though the farmer does not know how. Growth happens in silence, beyond our understanding or control. Daniel’s 21 days did not unfold with constant visible signs; much of the transformation was quiet—unseen roots deepening, heart slowly softening, perspective gently shifting. Today, pause to reflect with gratitude rather than scrutiny. Look back from Day 1 to now: notice one small, perhaps subtle change in your thoughts, attitudes, desires, or awareness of God’s presence. It may feel minor—a softer response to stress, a moment of unexpected peace, a returning hunger—but these are evidence of the Spirit’s quiet, steady work. Celebrate the hidden growth without demanding to see the full harvest yet. As this journey continues, trust that what God plants in secret, He faithfully brings to life in His time.

episodes.title: Day 14: Strength in Surrender | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Luke 22:42)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 14 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Strength in Surrender
Scripture: Luke 22:42 – "Not my will but yours be done."

Reflection: Surrender is not defeat—it is trust. God strengthens those who yield to Him.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Not My Will, But Yours
episodes.summary: In Luke 22:42, Jesus prays in the garden of Gethsemane: 'Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me; yet not my will but yours be done.' Facing the greatest trial, He models perfect surrender—not as weakness or resignation, but as profound trust that releases divine strength. Daniel’s 21 days were sustained by a similar yielding: he laid down his own comforts and agendas to align with God’s will. Today, you are invited to echo Jesus’ words in your own heart. Slowly speak the prayer 'Your will, not mine,' letting each word settle. Release one specific plan, outcome, worry, or desire you’ve been gripping tightly. In that act of surrender, you open space for God’s strength to flow where your own has reached its limit. Surrender is never defeat; it is the place where God’s power begins to work most freely in and through you. As this journey nears its midpoint, may this simple yielding become a source of quiet courage and renewed trust in His good and perfect will.

episodes.title: Day 15: God Has Heard You | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Daniel 10:12)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 15 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: God Has Heard You
Scripture: Daniel 10:12 – "From the first day that you set your mind to gain understanding, your words were heard."

Reflection: God hears you the moment you turn toward Him. Your prayer is never ignored.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Your Words Were Heard
episodes.summary: Daniel 10:12 unveils a stunning truth delivered by the angelic messenger: from the very first day Daniel set his heart to seek understanding and humbled himself before God, his words were heard. The delay in visible response was not due to divine deafness or distance—God was listening and moving from the outset. This midway point in your 21-day journey is a sacred reminder of your identity: you are one whose voice reaches heaven the instant you turn toward Him. No prayer is too small, too quiet, or too late. Today, write a short, honest prayer—whatever is on your heart—and then release it with confidence, trusting that it is fully received. You do not need to earn God’s attention; it is already yours in Christ. Let this assurance settle deeply: you are heard, you are seen, and you are loved. As these days continue, carry this truth as a steady anchor—your seeking has not gone unnoticed, and God is nearer than you feel.

episodes.title: Day 16: Do Not Be Afraid | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Daniel 10:19)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 16 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Do Not Be Afraid
Scripture: Daniel 10:19 – "Do not be afraid, you who are highly esteemed."

Reflection: God speaks courage into your fear. His presence strengthens trembling hearts.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Fear Not, You Are Highly Esteemed
episodes.summary: In Daniel 10:19, the heavenly messenger addresses Daniel with tender authority: 'Do not be afraid, you who are highly esteemed.' These words come after weeks of humble seeking, fasting, and waiting—yet the first thing God sends is not an explanation or a solution, but courage and affirmation of Daniel’s value in His sight. Fear often whispers that our struggles go unseen or that we are unworthy; God counters with the truth: you are cherished, noticed, and strengthened by His presence. Today, gently name one specific fear weighing on your heart—whether about the future, your worth, unanswered prayers, or something else—and place it before Jesus without trying to fix it. Simply offer it and receive His words: 'Do not be afraid.' Let His esteem over you quiet the trembling. As this journey draws toward its close, may courage rise not from your own resolve, but from the steady reality of God’s nearness and delight in you. You are not forgotten; you are highly esteemed.

episodes.title: Day 17: You Are Precious to God | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Isaiah 43:4)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 17 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: You Are Precious to God
Scripture: Isaiah 43:4 – "You are precious in my sight, and honored, and I love you."

Reflection: Your worth is not earned—it is given. God calls you precious.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Precious, Honored, Loved
episodes.summary: Isaiah 43:4 carries one of the most intimate declarations in Scripture: 'Because you are precious in my sight, and honored, and I love you.' Spoken to a people in exile, these words cut through shame, doubt, and fear with God’s unwavering affection. Your value is not measured by performance, productivity, answers received, or how 'strong' your faith feels—your preciousness is a gift, bestowed by the One who formed you and calls you His own. Daniel’s long season of seeking was met not with rejection but with the assurance that he was 'highly esteemed.' Today, you are invited to receive the same truth. Speak aloud, slowly and deliberately: 'I am precious to God.' Let the words settle over any inner resistance, comparison, or unbelief. Receive His love without needing to earn it. As these final days of the journey approach, carry this identity as a quiet, unshakable foundation: you are seen, honored, and deeply loved—exactly as you are.

episodes.title: Day 18: Strength for the Battle | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (Ephesians 6:10)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 18 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Strength for the Battle
Scripture: Ephesians 6:10 – "Be strong in the Lord and in the strength of his power."

Reflection: Spiritual battles are real, but God equips you. His strength becomes your armor.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.subtitle: Be Strong in His Power
episodes.summary: Ephesians 6:10 opens the famous passage on spiritual armor with a direct command and promise: 'Be strong in the Lord and in the strength of his power.' Paul does not urge self-generated toughness; he directs us to draw from God’s inexhaustible might. Daniel’s 21 days included unseen spiritual conflict—the messenger was delayed by opposition—yet God’s strength sustained him through every moment. Today, acknowledge that spiritual battles—whether doubt, temptation, discouragement, or unseen resistance—are real, but you are not left to fight in your own power. God Himself equips you; His strength becomes your armor, His presence your shield. Simply pray the words aloud or in your heart: 'Strengthen me, Lord.' Let this short, honest plea be your posture throughout the day. You do not need to feel mighty—only willing to receive what He freely gives. As these final days of the journey approach, stand firm not in your own resolve, but in the limitless power of the One who fights for you and within you.

episodes.title: Day 19: Understanding the Mission | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (John 20:21)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 19 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Understanding the Mission
Scripture: John 20:21 – "As the Father has sent me, so I send you."

Reflection: God doesn’t just comfort you—He sends you. Your life has purpose in His plan.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: As the Father Sent Me
episodes.summary: In John 20:21, the risen Jesus breathes peace on His disciples and then commissions them with these profound words: 'As the Father has sent me, so I send you.' The same sending that defined Jesus’ life—bringing love, truth, healing, and reconciliation—now flows through His followers. Daniel’s 21 days of seeking were never an end in themselves; they prepared him to receive understanding and continue faithfully in God’s purposes. After weeks of turning inward—intention, simplicity, stillness, surrender, hunger, perseverance, rest, and strength—today God gently turns your gaze outward. You are not merely comforted or strengthened for your own sake; you are sent. Pause and ask Jesus honestly: 'Where are You sending me today?' It may be a small, ordinary act—a kind word, a listening ear, a moment of forgiveness, a prayer for someone else—or something clearer that emerges over time. No mission is too small when it carries His love. As these final days approach, let this truth settle: your life is woven into God’s great sending. You are commissioned, equipped, and accompanied by the One who was sent first.

episodes.title: Day 20: Standing Firm in Grace | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (1 Peter 5:12)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 20 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Standing Firm in Grace
Scripture: 1 Peter 5:12 – "This is the true grace of God. Stand fast in it."

Reflection: Grace is not fragile. It holds you steady when life shakes.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: Stand Fast in True Grace
episodes.summary: In 1 Peter 5:12, after addressing suffering, trials, humility, and spiritual warfare, Peter concludes with a simple, powerful charge: 'This is the true grace of God. Stand fast in it.' Grace is not a weak consolation or a temporary boost—it is the firm, enduring foundation that holds believers steady when everything else trembles. Daniel stood firm through 21 days of waiting, weakness, and unseen battles because God’s grace sustained him from the first moment he turned toward Him. As your own journey nears its end, you are invited to do the same. Take one deep breath, speak the words aloud or in your heart—'Your grace is enough'—and let them anchor you. You do not need to strive harder, perform better, or feel stronger; grace is already carrying you. It is sufficient for every shake, every doubt, every ordinary moment, and every step forward. Stand fast—not in your own strength, but in the true, unbreakable grace of God that has been with you since Day 1 and will remain with you long after these 21 days.

episodes.title: Day 21: Sent Forth in Peace | 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey (John 14:27)
episodes.description:
Welcome to Day 21 of our 21-Day Prayer & Fasting Journey, inspired by Daniel's season of seeking God!

Today’s theme: Sent Forth in Peace
Scripture: John 14:27 – "Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you."

Reflection: You end these 21 days not empty but filled—with peace, clarity, and purpose. God sends you forth in His peace.

Follow the full 21-day devotional series here: https://seekgodwithdaniel.dailyaudio.cf
episodes.author: Eric Kouassi
episodes.subtitle: My Peace I Give to You
episodes.summary: In John 14:27, Jesus speaks words of profound farewell to His disciples on the eve of His crucifixion: 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.' This is not a fleeting feeling, but a deep, unshakable gift—His own peace—that sustains through every trial, transition, and sending. Over these 21 days, you have turned your heart toward God, simplified, stilled, waited, surrendered, hungered, persevered, rested, named distractions, released false strength, received His hearing and esteem, and been strengthened and sent. You do not conclude empty-handed; you go forth filled—with greater clarity, deeper trust, renewed desire, and now this divine peace that the world cannot give or take away. Today, receive Jesus’ gift afresh. Make the Sign of the Cross (or simply place a hand over your heart if that feels more natural), breathe in His peace, and step into your day—your life, your relationships, your mission—with the quiet confidence that His peace goes with you. These 21 days were never just about arrival; they were preparation for continuation. Carry His peace forward, let it guide your steps, guard your heart, and flow through you to others. You are sent, you are loved, and you are held in the perfect peace of Christ. Amen.
